creator: igirgen@FreeBSD.org
text:
When upgrading after xorg-7.x got into the ports tree, running portupgrade -aP, I got this:

Stale dependency: docbook-utils-0.6.14_2 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: dvipsk-tetex-5.95a_2 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: flock-0.7_5 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: ggv-2.12.0_1 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: html2ps-A4-1.0.b5_1,1 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: jadetex-3.13_2 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: kdegraphics-3.5.6_1 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)
Stale dependency: latex2html-2002.2.1_6 -> ghostscript-gpl-8.56_3 (print/ghostscript-gpl):
-> Deleted. (irrelevant)

I have ghostscript-afpl installed. I had assumed portupgrade would either ask me or choose it
over ghostscript-gnu, but deleting the dependency with the reason "irrelevant" seems false to me?

How-To-Repeat: 1. I had ghostscript-afpl installed, and lots of other ports all from before the xorg upgrade.
2. I followed the instructions in /usr/ports/UPGRADING about xorg-7.x
